sentences.base_language: I get up and wash my face.
I brush my teeth for two minutes.
sentences.grammarNote: When a verb-object compound (like 刷牙) has a duration, the verb is repeated: 刷牙刷两分钟 (brush teeth for two minutes). The pattern is: V+O + V + duration. This is because 了/duration attaches to a verb, not the compound.

sentences.base_language: My hair is long and black.
sentences.romanized: Wǒ de tóufa yòu cháng yòu hēi.
sentences.grammarNote: 又…又… (yòu…yòu…) = 'both … and …'. It connects two adjectives: 又长又黑 (both long and black), 又聪明又漂亮 (both smart and beautiful). Always two qualities, both applied to the same subject.
穿上 = 'put on (clothes)'. 上 (shàng) as a directional complement means the action results in something being on: 穿上 (put on), 戴上 (put on accessories). The opposite is 穿下/脱下 (take off clothing).

准备好了 = 'have gotten ready' (准备 + 好 resultative complement + 了 change of state). 好 as a result complement means 'successfully done / properly done': 写好了 (finished writing), 做好了 (done/ready). 终于 = 'finally'.

出门 = 'go out the door / leave home'. The sentence-final 了 signals a change of state: 'I (have now) left the house.' This use of 了 is very common to announce that something just happened or a new situation has begun.
